name: Chocolate-Tipped Butter Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup finely chopped pecans or almonds (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the butter and powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Stir in the vanilla extract.
3. Gradually add the flour to the mixture and mix well.
4. Shape the dough into 2 1/2 inch x 1/2 inch sticks.
5. Place the sticks on ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Flatten three-quarters of each cookie lengthwise with the tines of a fork until they are about 1/4 inch thick.
7. Bake at 350°F for 12 to 14 minutes or until lightly golden.
8. Allow cookies to cool completely.
9. In a double boiler, melt chocolate and shortening over low heat, stirring occasionally.
10. Remove the double boiler from the heat and keep the chocolate mixture over hot water to maintain its consistency.
11. Dip the unflattened ends of the cookies into the warm chocolate, coating both sides.
12. Roll the tips in chopped nuts if using.
13. Place the cookies on wire racks to set the chocolate.
14. Store the cooled cookies in an airtight container at cool room temperature.
